findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Analysis/Bochner/CharFunPositiveDefinite.lean:43-76` states `charFun_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg` with `[SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [MeasurableSpace E] [OpensMeasurableSpace E] [IsFiniteMeasure μ]`; Mathlib `CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:130-171` has `charFun_apply`/`norm_charFun_le` before any normed or Borel assumptions.
findings.fix: Split the file into sections: state `fourierConvention`, `fourierConvention_eq_charFun`, norm lemmas, and `fourierConvention_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg` under `[SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [MeasurableSpace E]`, adding only `[OpensMeasurableSpace E] [IsFiniteMeasure μ]` for positive-definiteness; reserve `[NormedAddCommGroup E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]` for continuity.
findings.issue: The basic conversion/norm/positive-definite API is stated under `[NormedAddCommGroup E]`, and the positive-definite theorem also inherits `[BorelSpace E]` from line 108, although the underlying `charFun` positive-definite bridge is available at the seminormed/open-measurable level.

summary: The Fourier bridge is mostly at the intended API level, but the non-continuity declarations inherit stronger topology assumptions than the existing characteristic-function API needs.
